annotations.label: Determinant (informal)

anchor.snippet: the determinant is a scalar -valued function of the entries of a square matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.det` defines the determinant of a square matrix over a commutative ring.

annotations.label: Nonzero determinant iff invertible

anchor.snippet: the determinant is nonzero if and only if the matrix is invertible

annotations.note: Stated as `IsUnit A ↔ IsUnit A.det`, which over a field is `A invertible ↔ det A ≠ 0`.

annotations.label: Determinant characterized by product and triangular rules

anchor.snippet: the determinant of a product of matrices is the product of their determinants

annotations.note: `Matrix.det_mul` and `Matrix.det_of_upperTriangular` together give the product and triangular rules.

annotations.label: Axiomatic characterization (four properties)

anchor.snippet: The determinant of the identity matrix is 1

annotations.note: `detRowAlternating` packages the alternating-multilinear properties and `det_one` gives identity = 1, but the precise four-property axiomatic characterization is not stated as a single theorem.

annotations.label: Similarity invariance of the determinant

anchor.snippet: The determinant is invariant under matrix similarity

annotations.note: `Matrix.det_conj` shows `det (M * N * M⁻¹) = det N` when `M` is a unit.

annotations.label: Determinant of a 2×2 matrix

anchor.snippet: The determinant of a 2 × 2 matrix

annotations.note: `det_fin_two` gives `det A = A 0 0 * A 1 1 - A 0 1 * A 1 0`.

annotations.label: Identity matrix has determinant 1

anchor.snippet: first, the determinant of the identity matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.det_one : det (1 : Matrix n n R) = 1`.

annotations.label: Two equal rows give determinant zero

anchor.snippet: the determinant is zero if two rows are the same

annotations.note: `det_zero_of_row_eq` shows that a repeated row makes the determinant zero.

annotations.label: Scaling a column scales the determinant

anchor.snippet: if any column is multiplied by some number

annotations.note: `det_updateCol_smul` scales the determinant by `s` when one column is scaled by `s`.

annotations.label: |ad−bc| is parallelogram area

anchor.snippet: The absolute value of ad − bc is the area of the parallelogram

annotations.note: Mathlib relates `|det|` to volume of `parallelepiped` in general dimension, but no specific 2D `|ad-bc|` = area statement is isolated.

annotations.label: Signed area of parallelogram

anchor.snippet: The absolute value of the determinant together with the sign becomes the signed area

annotations.note: The 2D oriented `areaForm` in `InnerProductSpace.TwoDim` realizes signed area via `Matrix.det_fin_two`, though signed-area-of-parallelogram is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Determinant as n-dimensional signed volume

anchor.snippet: The determinant gives the signed n -dimensional volume of this parallelotope

annotations.note: `Orientation.volumeForm` gives signed volume via determinants, but signed-volume-of-parallelotope is not stated verbatim.

annotations.label: Notation for the determinant

anchor.snippet: The determinant of A is denoted by det( A )

annotations.note: Written as `Matrix.det A` or `A.det`.

annotations.label: Leibniz formula for 3×3 determinant

anchor.snippet: The Leibniz formula for the determinant of a 3 × 3 matrix

annotations.note: `det_fin_three` gives the explicit six-term expansion.

annotations.label: Rule of Sarrus

anchor.snippet: The rule of Sarrus is a mnemonic for the expanded form

annotations.note: Sarrus's mnemonic is the same identity as `det_fin_three`.

annotations.label: Permutation of a finite set

anchor.snippet: A permutation of the set

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α := α ≃ α` formalizes permutations of any (finite) type.

annotations.label: Leibniz formula for n×n determinant

anchor.snippet: the Leibniz formula for its determinant is, using sigma notation for the sum

annotations.note: `det_apply` states `M.det = ∑ σ, sign σ • ∏ i, M (σ i) i`.

annotations.label: Levi-Civita symbol

anchor.snippet: The Levi-Civita symbol

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.sign` plays the role of the Levi-Civita symbol on permutations, though no `LeviCivita` symbol exists per se.

annotations.label: Determinant is multilinear in columns

anchor.snippet: The determinant is multilinear

annotations.note: `detRowAlternating` exhibits `det` as an `AlternatingMap`, which is in particular multilinear.

annotations.label: Determinant is alternating

anchor.snippet: The determinant is alternating

annotations.note: Recorded directly in the type of `detRowAlternating : (n → R) [⋀^n]→ₗ[R] R`.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of determinant from three properties

anchor.snippet: there is exactly one function that assigns to any

annotations.note: The 1-dimensional space of top alternating multilinear forms is developed, but uniqueness of `det` from the three axioms is not stated as a single named theorem.

annotations.label: Determinant is homogeneous

anchor.snippet: The determinant is a homogeneous function

annotations.note: `det_smul` shows `det (c • A) = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A`.

anchors.snippet: The determinant is a homogeneous function

annotations.label: Column swap multiplies determinant by −1

anchor.snippet: Interchanging any pair of columns of a matrix multiplies its determinant by

annotations.note: `det_permute` shows permuting columns by σ multiplies the determinant by `sign σ`; a swap gives `-1`.

anchors.snippet: Interchanging any pair of columns of a matrix multiplies its determinant by

annotations.label: Linearly dependent columns give determinant 0

anchor.snippet: If some column can be expressed as a linear combination of the other columns

annotations.note: Directly states `¬ LinearIndependent → det = 0`.

annotations.label: Column-addition leaves determinant unchanged

anchor.snippet: Adding a scalar multiple of one column to another column does not change the value of the determinant

annotations.note: `det_updateCol_add_smul_self` shows adding `c • A j` to column `i ≠ j` leaves the determinant unchanged.

annotations.label: Triangular matrix determinant equals product of diagonal

anchor.snippet: its determinant equals the product of the diagonal entries

annotations.note: `det_of_upperTriangular` (and its lower analogue) give the product-of-diagonal formula.

anchors.snippet: its determinant equals the product of the diagonal entries

annotations.label: Determinant via Gaussian elimination

anchor.snippet: The following concrete example illustrates the computation of the determinant

annotations.note: A worked numerical example, not a theorem; nothing to formalize.

annotations.label: Determinant equals determinant of transpose

anchor.snippet: The determinant of the transpose

annotations.note: `det_transpose : Mᵀ.det = M.det`.

annotations.label: Multiplicativity of the determinant

anchor.snippet: The determinant is a multiplicative map

annotations.note: `det_mul : det (M * N) = det M * det N`; packaged also as `Matrix.detMonoidHom`.

annotations.label: Invertibility iff nonzero determinant (over a field)

anchor.snippet: is invertible precisely if its determinant is nonzero

annotations.note: Stated over commutative rings: `IsUnit A ↔ IsUnit A.det`, which over a field becomes `det A ≠ 0`.

annotations.label: Determinant of an inverse

anchor.snippet: the determinant of the inverse matrix is given by

annotations.note: `det_nonsing_inv : A⁻¹.det = A.det⁻¹ʳ` (Ring.inverse), plus `det_invOf` for `Invertible`.

annotations.label: det as group homomorphism GL_n → field×

anchor.snippet: it is in fact a group homomorphism

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup.det : GLₙ R →* Rˣ` is the determinant as a group homomorphism.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Binet formula

anchor.snippet: The Cauchy–Binet formula is a generalization

annotations.note: No Cauchy–Binet formula for products of non-square matrices was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laplace expansion along a row

anchor.snippet: which is called the Laplace expansion along the i th row

annotations.note: `det_succ_row` (and `det_succ_column`) give the Laplace expansion for `Fin (n+1)` matrices.

annotations.label: Minor and cofactor of a matrix

anchor.snippet: Laplace expansion expresses the determinant of a matrix

annotations.note: Minors are taken via `Matrix.submatrix` (used inside `det_succ_row`), but no named `cofactor` or `minor` definitions exist.

annotations.label: Adjugate matrix

anchor.snippet: The adjugate matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.adjugate` defines the classical adjugate (transpose of the cofactor matrix).

annotations.label: Inverse via adjugate matrix

anchor.snippet: Thus the adjugate matrix can be used for expressing the inverse

annotations.note: `mul_adjugate`/`adjugate_mul` give `A * adjugate A = det A • 1`, and `Matrix.inv_def` expresses `A⁻¹` using the adjugate.

annotations.label: Block-matrix determinant formula

anchor.snippet: continues to hold, under appropriate further assumptions, for a block matrix

annotations.note: `det_fromBlocks_zero₂₁`/`zero₁₂` and Schur-complement formulas in `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.SchurComplement` cover block determinants.

annotations.label: Sylvester's determinant theorem

anchor.snippet: Sylvester's determinant theorem states that for A

annotations.note: `det_one_add_mul_comm` (Weinstein–Aronszajn) gives `det(1 + AB) = det(1 + BA)` for rectangular `A`, `B`.

annotations.label: Determinant inequality for positive semidefinite matrices

anchor.snippet: for positive semidefinite matrices

annotations.note: Mathlib has PSD matrix infrastructure (`Matrix.PosSemidef`) but no `det(A + B) ≥ det A + det B` style inequality was found.

annotations.label: Brunn–Minkowski concavity of nth-root determinant

anchor.snippet: Brunn–Minkowski theorem implies that the n th root of determinant is a concave function

annotations.note: Concavity of `(det A)^(1/n)` on PSD matrices was not located.

anchors.snippet: Brunn–Minkowski theorem implies that the n th root of determinant is a concave function

annotations.label: Sum identity for 2×2 determinants

anchor.snippet: the determinant of the sum can be written in terms of determinants and traces

annotations.note: The identity `det(A+B) = det A + det B + tr A tr B − tr(AB)` for 2×2 matrices was not found.

annotations.label: Determinant equals product of eigenvalues

anchor.snippet: the determinant of A is equal to the product of these eigenvalues

annotations.note: `det_eq_prod_roots_charpoly` (alg. closed) and `IsHermitian.det_eq_prod_eigenvalues` give this identity.

annotations.label: Pseudo-determinant

anchor.snippet: The product of all non-zero eigenvalues is referred to as pseudo-determinant

annotations.note: No pseudo-determinant was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characteristic polynomial

anchor.snippet: The characteristic polynomial is defined as

annotations.note: `Matrix.charpoly A := det (charmatrix A)`.

annotations.label: Sylvester's criterion for positive definiteness

anchor.snippet: Sylvester's criterion asserts that this is equivalent to the determinants of the submatrices

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Matrix.PosDef` but no Sylvester's-criterion characterization via leading principal minors was found.

annotations.label: det(exp A) = exp(tr A)

anchor.snippet: Here exp( A ) denotes the matrix exponential of A

annotations.note: No `det_exp = exp_trace` style identity was located in `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Algebra.MatrixExponential`.

annotations.label: Trace bounds on the log determinant

anchor.snippet: the trace operator gives the following tight lower and upper bounds on the log determinant

annotations.note: No log-determinant/trace inequalities were located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobi's formula for derivative of determinant

anchor.snippet: Its derivative can be expressed using Jacobi's formula

annotations.note: A specialized polynomial-derivative version exists; the general analytic `d/dt det A(t) = tr(adj A · A')` Jacobi formula was not found.

annotations.label: Cramer's rule

anchor.snippet: the solution is given by Cramer's rule

annotations.note: `Matrix.cramer` and `Matrix.mulVec_cramer` formalize Cramer's rule.

annotations.label: Determinant detects linear dependence of columns

anchor.snippet: Determinants can be used to characterize linearly dependent vectors

annotations.note: Together with `linearIndependent_cols_of_det_ne_zero` this gives the iff over a domain.

annotations.label: Wronskian

anchor.snippet: the Wronskian is defined to be

annotations.note: No Wronskian definition was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cross product as a formal 3×3 determinant

anchor.snippet: The computation of a cross product is equivalent to finding the formal determinant

annotations.note: `crossProduct` is defined and appears (e.g. in `triple_product_eq_det`-style lemmas using `det_fin_three`) but it is not defined or characterized as a 'formal' 3×3 determinant explicitly.

anchors.snippet: The computation of a cross product is equivalent to finding the formal determinant

annotations.label: Sign of determinant gives basis orientation

anchor.snippet: the sign of the determinant determines whether the orientation of the basis is consistent

annotations.note: `orientation_eq_iff_det_pos` characterizes equality of orientations via positivity of the change-of-basis determinant.

annotations.label: |det| equals volume of parallelepiped

anchor.snippet: the absolute value of the determinant of real vectors is equal to the volume of the parallelepiped

annotations.note: `OrthonormalBasis.volume_parallelepiped` and `Basis.addHaar` relate parallelepiped volume to determinants but the bare `|det| = volume` identity for arbitrary vectors is not a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Jacobian matrix

anchor.snippet: the Jacobian matrix is the n × n matrix whose entries are given by the partial derivatives

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the coordinate-free `fderiv` rather than an explicit Jacobian matrix; `(fderiv 𝕜 f x).det` plays the Jacobian determinant role.

annotations.label: Change of variables via Jacobian determinant

anchor.snippet: the higher-dimensional version of integration by substitution

annotations.note: `integral_image_eq_integral_abs_det_fderiv_smul` is the n-dimensional change-of-variables formula.

annotations.label: Triangle area via determinant

anchor.snippet: The area of a triangle whose vertices are

annotations.note: No specific triangle-area-via-3×3-determinant lemma was located.

annotations.label: Similar matrices have the same determinant

anchor.snippet: similar matrices have the same determinant

annotations.note: `det_conj h N : det (M * N * M⁻¹) = det N` when `M` is a unit.

annotations.label: Determinant of a linear endomorphism

anchor.snippet: The determinant of a linear transformation

annotations.note: `LinearMap.det : (M →ₗ[R] M) →* R` is the basis-independent determinant of an endomorphism.

annotations.label: Determinant over a commutative ring

anchor.snippet: The above definition of the determinant using the Leibniz rule works more generally

annotations.note: `Matrix.det` is defined for any matrix over a `CommRing R`.

annotations.label: Invertibility over commutative rings

anchor.snippet: is invertible (in the sense that there is an inverse matrix whose entries are in

annotations.note: `IsUnit A ↔ IsUnit A.det` over a commutative ring.

annotations.label: Unimodular matrix

anchor.snippet: Such a matrix is called unimodular

annotations.note: Only the stronger 'totally unimodular' notion is defined; a `IsUnimodular := IsUnit A.det` definition was not found as a standalone name.

annotations.label: Coordinate-free determinant via exterior power

anchor.snippet: can be formulated in a coordinate-free manner by considering

annotations.note: `LinearMap.det` is coordinate-free but Mathlib defines it via `Matrix.det` after choosing a basis rather than via the top exterior power.

annotations.label: Berezin integral expression for the determinant

anchor.snippet: the determinant can be written using the somewhat surprising notation of the Berezin integral

annotations.note: No Berezin-integral formalism was located.

annotations.label: Pfaffian as square-root of determinant

anchor.snippet: is the square-root of the determinant

annotations.note: No Pfaffian definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Permanent of a matrix

anchor.snippet: the permanent of a matrix is defined as the determinant

annotations.note: `Matrix.permanent` defines the permanent as a sum over permutations without signs.

annotations.label: Immanant

anchor.snippet: The immanant generalizes both by introducing a character

annotations.note: No immanant definition was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Determinant for finite-dimensional algebras

anchor.snippet: there is a determinant map

annotations.note: `Algebra.norm` is the determinant of multiplication-by-x on a finite free algebra; coincides with the algebra-norm determinant.

annotations.label: Fredholm determinant

anchor.snippet: The Fredholm determinant defines the determinant for operators known as trace class operators

annotations.note: No Fredholm determinant was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fuglede−Kadison determinant

anchor.snippet: one may define a positive real-valued determinant called the Fuglede−Kadison determinant

annotations.note: No Fuglede–Kadison determinant was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incompatibility over non-commutative rings

anchor.snippet: multilinearity and alternating properties are incompatible

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Matrix.det` only over commutative rings; no formal incompatibility no-go theorem is stated.
